content.text: Contoso Retail is committed to providing a safe and healthful workplace for every team member. Safety is not optional — it is a core value and everyone's responsibility. This handbook outlines your OSHA rights, key safety responsibilities, and the fundamental safety practices that govern our operations.
Under the Occupational Safety and Health Act (OSHA), every worker has the right to:

items: Work in conditions that do not pose a risk of serious harm.
Receive information and training about workplace hazards, methods to prevent harm, and OSHA standards.
Review records of work-related injuries and illnesses.
File a confidential complaint with OSHA and request an inspection if you believe there is a serious hazard.
Exercise your rights under OSHA without retaliation or discrimination from your employer.

content.text: Contoso Retail strictly prohibits retaliation against any team member who reports a safety concern, files an OSHA complaint, or participates in an OSHA inspection.
Safety is a shared responsibility across all levels of the organization:

rows: Team Member
Follow all safety rules; wear required PPE; report hazards and incidents immediately; participate in safety training; never bypass safeguards.
Lead Team Member
Conduct daily zone safety checks; reinforce PPE compliance; ensure new members follow safety procedures; escalate hazards promptly.
Supervisor
Conduct pre-shift safety walk; investigate all incidents and near-misses; ensure 100% training completion; enforce safety standards consistently.
Safety Coordinator
Maintain safety training calendar; conduct inspections; manage incident records; coordinate OSHA compliance.
Warehouse Manager
Allocate resources for safety programs; review incident trends; ensure regulatory compliance; champion safety culture.

content.text: Identifying hazards before they cause injury is the foundation of our safety program. Common warehouse hazards and how to address them:

rows: Slip/Trip/Fall
Spills, floor debris, uneven surfaces, open cord paths
Clean immediately; place wet floor signs; report to supervisor
Struck-By
Moving forklifts, falling objects, swinging dock doors
Stay in pedestrian lanes; never walk under raised loads
Caught-In/Between
Conveyor belts, pallet jacks, racking pinch points
Keep hands clear; follow LOTO procedures for maintenance
Ergonomic
Repetitive motion, heavy lifting, awkward postures
Use team-lift for items >50 lbs; use mechanical assists
Chemical
Cleaning agents, battery acid (forklift), aerosols
Read SDS; wear appropriate PPE; store per labeling
Electrical
Exposed wiring, overloaded outlets, wet equipment
Do not use damaged equipment; report to maintenance

content.text: If you identify a hazard that you cannot immediately correct, place a warning cone or barrier, notify your supervisor, and log it using the Warehouse Assistant agent or the facility's hazard log binder.
Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) procedures protect workers from unexpected energization or startup of machinery during maintenance or servicing. Only trained and authorized employees may perform LOTO.

items: Notify all affected employees that equipment will be shut down.
Identify all energy sources (electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, gravity).
Shut down the equipment using normal stopping procedures.
Isolate all energy sources using the designated lockout points.
Apply your personal lock and tag to each energy isolation point. Each worker uses their own lock.
Release or restrain all stored energy (bleed lines, block raised parts, discharge capacitors).
Verify the equipment is de-energized by attempting a normal startup — ensure no one is in the danger zone before testing.
After work is complete: ensure all workers are clear, remove locks and tags in reverse order, notify affected employees, and restore energy.

content.text: Never remove another worker's lock. If a lock cannot be accounted for, notify the supervisor immediately. Do not attempt to defeat a lockout.
A clean warehouse is a safe warehouse. All team members are responsible for maintaining housekeeping standards in their assigned zones:

items: Keep aisles clear of pallets, cardboard, shrink wrap, and all other obstructions at all times.
Break down and bale cardboard immediately after unpacking — do not allow accumulation on the floor.
Clean up spills immediately using the appropriate supplies from the spill station. For chemical spills, refer to the SDS and use PPE.
Return equipment (pallet jacks, carts, hand trucks) to designated storage areas after each use.
Dispose of trash in designated bins — never in aisles, at dock doors, or against racking.
Report damaged racking, flooring, or dock equipment to your supervisor — do not continue using damaged equipment.
End-of-shift sweep of your zone is required. Supervisor confirms completion during handoff.

content.text: All injuries, illnesses, and near-misses must be reported immediately — no matter how minor they seem. Delayed reporting can affect your workers' compensation eligibility and our ability to prevent future incidents. See the Incident & Near-Miss Reporting Policy document for full details.

items: Report any injury to your supervisor immediately.
Seek first aid at the first aid station (Zone F). For serious injuries, call 911 first.
Complete an Incident Report form with your supervisor before end of shift.
Do not alter the scene of a serious incident until supervisors and safety personnel have assessed it.
Near-misses (close calls with no injury) must also be reported — they are leading indicators of future incidents.
